provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Animal Welfare (Impounded Animal) Regulations, 2020.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires— “ Act ” means the Animal Welfare Act; [Cap 154] “ animal ” means any vertebrates or invertebrates other than human being; “ animal welfare inspector ” means any registered veterinarian, enrolled paraprofessional, enlisted paraprofessional assistant, livestock officer or livestock field officer appointed to be an animal welfare inspector and every zoo sanitary inspector and member of the Police Force or TANAPA Police Officers (Police USU) above the level of Inspector; “ animal welfare society ” means an organization established and registered for the purpose of preventing cruelty and promotion of animal welfare; “ companion animal ” has the meaning ascribed to it under the Act ; “ complainant ” means any person, authority or institution that has seized an animal ; “ court ” means any court in the United Republic, of competent jurisdiction; “ impound ” means to seize and take legal custody of animals; “ kennel ” means any structure designed specifically for keeping pets; “ seized animal ” means any held animal found in a restricted area ; “ restricted area ” means lands, roads, farms, buildings, national parks, game reserve, wildlife management area or any other structures public or privately owned where animals are not allowed without a permit issued under any written laws; “ seizure ” means to take hold of animals found in a restricted area ; and “ stray animal ” means any animal that has moved away from a group, owner, place or designated area to a restricted area . Part II – Establishment of animal pound

provisions.title: Requirements for animal pound
provisions.content: (1) Any seized or stray animal shall be kept in an animal pound or facility that is acceptable under these Regulations. (2) Subject to sub-regulation (1) , animal pound facilities shall have the following requirements for different species— (a) fenced compound, treatment crush, ramp, watering trough, feeding trough and shade for cattle, sheep, goats, pigs, donkeys and other domesticated species; (b) kennels, cages, shelter, feeders and drinkers for poultry, dogs, cats and other pet species. (3) An animal pound or facility shall be accessible to the owner of the animals, animal welfare inspector and any other Government officer upon notification.

provisions.title: Establishment and operation of animal pound
provisions.content: (1) A person who intends to establish and operate an animal pound shall apply for the animal pound permit to the District Veterinary Officer after complying with the conditions prescribed under these Regulations. (2) The District Veterinary Officer shall, upon being satisfied that the applicant has complied with all the conditions necessary for the establishment of the animal pound, issue an animal pound permit as set out in Form A of the First Schedule to these Regulations. (3) The applicant shall pay the animal pound permit fees to the District Council as specified in the Second Schedule to these Regulations. (4) Subject to sub-regulation (1) , the applicant shall comply with the following conditions— (a) the location shall be approved by the District Veterinary Officer and assure the sanitation, health, safety and comfort of the community and animals; (b) that the records of the impounded animals are accurately kept and maintained; and (c) that he keeps and maintains records for animal health, loss, births, deaths and other services rendered and submit a monthly report to the District Veterinary Officer. (5) The District Veterinary Officer may reject the application for the permit and he shall inform the applicant the reasons thereof. (6) The animal pound owner shall, when required and at any time, avail the records referred to under sub-regulation (4)(a) and (b) to the animal welfare inspector or any other Government officer for inspection. (7) The animal pound owner shall, where there is a disease outbreak or death of an animal , report immediately to the District Veterinary Officer. Part III – Duties

provisions.title: Duties of District Veterinary Officers
provisions.content: The District Veterinary Officer shall have duties to— (a) enforce implementation of the Act and its Regulations in their respective Districts; (b) enforce establishment, registration, monitoring of animal pound facilities and performance of impounded animals; (c) maintain the District list of areas of animal pound facilities; (d) certify treatments, births and deaths of impounded animals and prepare reports; (e) compile reports from animal pound facilities and submit to Zonal Veterinary Centre; and (f) perform any other duties as may be directed by the competent authority. [Cap. 154]

provisions.title: Duties of animal welfare inspectors
provisions.content: The animal welfare inspector shall have duties to— (a) implement the Act under the supervision of the District Veterinary Officer; (b) compile reports from animal pound facilities and submit to the District Veterinary Officer; (c) protect an animal from ill treatment and necessary suffering during loading, passage, landing and pounding; and (d) perform any other duties as may be directed by the immediate supervisor or District Veterinary Officer. [Cap. 154]

provisions.title: Duty to provide information
provisions.content: (1) The complainant shall notify the court during on-going legal proceedings on the status of death, loss or birth of the impounded animal . (2) The pound owner shall notify the animal welfare inspector and the animal owner in case of death, loss or birth. (3) The animal pound owner and the complainant shall fill in Form B and Form C of the First Schedule to these Regulations that can be used as evidence. Part IV – Seizing and impounding

provisions.title: Animal seizure and impounding
provisions.content: (1) Any animal seized or impounded shall be kept in accordance with conditions set out in these Regulations. (2) Any animal found straying within the restricted areas, abused animals or any other animal whose owners have violated other laws shall be seized or impounded in a permitted animal pound facility. (3) Subject to sub-regulation (2) , an animal shall be impounded or seized in a manner that does not cause injury or undue suffering or distress. (4) Seizure or impounding of animals may be conducted by animal welfare inspector or any other officer responsible for livestock, Village or Ward Executive Officer or Mtaa Executive Officer or the ten cell leader. (5) The owner of an animal that is found in a restricted area or straying outside the owner’s confinement and care commits an offence.

provisions.title: Seizure by complainant
provisions.content: (1) The complainant shall, before impounding of an animal and while observing animal rights under regulation 14(1) , seize the animal and notify the animal welfare inspector or any other officer responsible for livestock, Village or Ward Executive Officer or Mtaa Executive Officer from his area of jurisdiction within twenty four hours of such seizure . (2) The notification under sub-regulation (1) , shall contain information on the place of seizure , type, number of animals, any identification mark, photograph, video, time and or destruction caused by the animal . (3) Animal welfare inspector or any other officer responsible for livestock, Village or Ward Executive Officer or Mtaa Executive Officer shall, upon notification of the seizure and in collaboration with the complainant , make sure that the seized animals are kept in a permitted pound. (4) The animal welfare inspector shall, within twenty four hours after receiving the notification, verify the information, prepare a summary report of impounded animals as set out in Form D of the First Schedule to these Regulations and submit it to the District Veterinary Officer for implementation. (5) Subject to sub-regulation (4) , in the absence of the animal welfare inspector , any other officer responsible for livestock, the Village or Ward Executive Officer or Mtaa Executive Officer may continue with implementation procedures, prepare and submit a report to the animal welfare inspector who shall verify it and submit to the District Veterinary Officer. (6) The District Veterinary officer shall provide report to the Regional Veterinary Officer, Incharge of Zonal Veterinary Centres and the Director within forty eight hours for implementation. (7) A person shall not transport an impounded or seized animal in a manner that is likely to cause pain, injury or undue suffering or distress to the animal .

provisions.title: Known owner of impounded animals
provisions.content: (1) Where the owner of impounded animals is known, the animal welfare inspector shall require the owner to complete and sign the compliance or seizure form set out in the First Schedule to these Regulations. (2) The compliance or seizure form shall be filled in triplicate and signed by the owner, complainant and animal welfare inspector . (3) The filled compliance or seizure forms may be used as evidence of the offence during legal procedures. (4) The complainant shall apply to the Court for release of impounded animals within seventy two hours. (5) Upon the Court order, the impounded animals shall be released and returned to the owner.

provisions.title: Unknown owner of impounded animals
provisions.content: (1) Where the owner of the impounded animals is not known, the animal welfare inspector or any other officer responsible for livestock, Village or Ward Executive Officer or Mtaa Executive Officer shall make public announcement of the impounded animals in a written form and place it in mtaa, ward or village offices or any other public space or use any other local means of announcement to assist in locating the owner for thirty days. (2) Any person claiming ownership of the animals shall provide an introduction letter from Village or Mtaa or Ward Executive Officer or police loss report with the details of number of lost animals and any identification mark. (3) A person claiming to have lost animals, shall be allowed by the owner of the restricted area to enter and identify the impounded animals accompanied by the animal welfare inspector or any other officer responsible for livestock, Village or Ward Executive Officer or Mtaa Executive Officer after presenting all relevant documents. (4) Where the owner does not appear to make claim on the animals within thirty days during public announcement, the complainant shall apply for court order to confiscate such animals.

provisions.title: Release of impounded animals
provisions.content: (1) A person who successful proved the ownership of impounded animal , shall pay fees for seizure , keeping of such animal and any other costs associated with the impounded animal to the animal pound owner as specified in the Third Schedule to these Regulations. (2) During the release of impounded animals, the animal pound owner, the complainant and the animal owner shall fill in the pound release form set out in Form C of the First Schedule to these Regulations. Part V – Management of pounded animal

provisions.title: Sheltering of pounded animals
provisions.content: (1) Animals pounded shall be kept in a structure designed specifically for the use of keeping animals which shall comply with the following conditions: (a) fenced shelter that can protect animals from predators; (b) ensure safety of animals from theft; (c) ensure sanitation, good health and comfort; (d) suitable shelter size, composed to accommodate the animal and allow maintenance of body heat; (e) sufficient quantity of suitable beddings to provide insulation; and (f) ensure favourable conditions to prevent exposure to extreme weather conditions. (2) Without prejudice to sub-regulation (1) animals of different species, age, size and health status shall be kept separately.

provisions.title: Feeding
provisions.content: (1) Impounded animals shall be provided with safe, adequate and quality food to meet their normal daily requirements according to species. (2) Feeders shall be used and be readily accessible to all animals, be located in a better position possibly to minimize any type of contamination and be protected from adverse weather. (3) Feeders shall be of material that can be easily cleaned. (4) Animals shall be given fresh water at all times and water receptacles shall be kept clean.

provisions.title: Health care
provisions.content: (1) The owner of animal pound shall, in order to maintain good health and general welfare of the animals, provide veterinary care for animals within their control. (2) Notwithstanding sub-regulation (1) , all impounded animals retained beyond seven days shall— (a) be vaccinated against strategic diseases in a manner provided for in the Animal Diseases (Vaccines and Vaccination) Regulations, or as recommended by District Veterinary Officer; and [GN. No. 180 of 2020] (b) be applied with acaricides to kill parasites on the animals in a manner provided for under the Animal Diseases (Acaricides Application and Management) Regulations, so as to protect them from vectors and vector-borne diseases. [GN. No. 955 of 2019]

provisions.title: Relocation of impounded animals
provisions.content: A person shall not relocate any impounded animals without a written approval and movement permit from the Director. Part VI – Miscellaneous provisions

provisions.title: Offences and penalties
provisions.content: (1) Any person who contravenes or fails to comply with any of the provisions of these Regulations commits an offence and shall, upon conviction, be liable to a fine of not less than one hundred thousand shillings but not exceeding one million shillings or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding one month or to both such a fine and imprisonment. (2) Any person who unlawfully kills or causes bodily injuries to any animal by means of sharp objects, beating, poison or gun commits an offence and shall, upon conviction, be liable to a fine of not less than one hundred thousand shillings but not exceeding one million shillings or to imprisonment for a term of not less than six months but not exceeding one year or to both and in addition the court shall order payment of compensation for the killed or injured animal to the owner. (3) Where a Court is satisfied that an offence under sub-regulation (1) has been committed in respect of an animal , whether or not any person has been convicted in respect of such offence, the Court may order payment of compensation to the victim so affected.

provisions.title: Compounding of offences
provisions.content: (1) Where a person admits in writing that he has committed an offence under these Regulations, the Director or any authorized officer may, at any time prior to the commencement of the proceedings by a court of competent jurisdiction, compound such offence and order such person to pay a sum of money not exceeding five hundred thousand Tanzania shillings in respect of the offences provided for in the Fourth Schedule to these Regulations. (2) Subject to sub-regulation (1) a person whose offence is compounded shall fill a compounding form prescribed in the Fifth Schedule to these Regulations and make payment within 14 days.

provisions.title: Revocation
provisions.content: The Animal Welfare (Animal Pound) Regulations, 2010 are hereby revoked. [ GN No. 184 of 2010 ] First Schedule Forms [Please note: Forms have not been reproduced. Please refer to the publication document.] Second Schedule (Made under regulation 4 (3)) Pound permit fees Animal species Permit fee (Tshs) Retention fee (per year) 1. Cattle 100,000 10,000 2. Sheep/Goat 20,000 5,000 3. Horse/Donkey 100,000 10,000 4. Camel 100,000 10,000 5. Dog/Cat 100,000 10,000 6. Poultry 20,000 5,000 7. Mixed domestic ruminants 150,000 20,000 Third Schedule (Made under regulation 12 (1)) Fees for impound and seizure Animal species Daily impounding fees per head (Tshs) Seizure fees charged once per head (Tshs) 1. Cattle 1000 10,000 2. Shhep/Goat 500 5,000 3. Horse/Donkey 1000 10,000 4. Camel 1000 10,000 5. Dog/Cat 500 5,000 6. Poultry 100 1,000 Fourth Schedule (Made under regulation 18(1)) List of offences to be compounded 1. Transportation of animals in a manner that causes pain or discomfort. 2. Not carrying a permit and other documents relevant for movement of animal. 3. Delay or obstruction of an animal welfare inspector to perform his power. 4. Improper maintenance and fabrication of impounded animal records. 5. Failure to provide feeds, water, shelter and health care. 6. Unlawful killing or cause of bodily injuries to any animal by means of sharp objects, animal abuse, poison or gun. Fifth Schedule (Made under regulation 18(2)) Compounding of offence form (To be filled in triplicate) [Please note: Form has not been reproduced.
